identity.sql: SELECT a, b FROM t WHERE x > 1
SELECT "MyColumn" FROM "MyTable"
SELECT "SELECT" FROM "ORDER"
SELECT 'it''s' AS val FROM t
identity.description: Escaped single quote in string literal

identity.sql: SELECT NULL AS x FROM t
SELECT dept, COUNT(*) FROM employees GROUP BY dept ORDER BY dept
SELECT a.x, b.y FROM a INNER JOIN b ON a.id = b.id
SELECT a.x, b.y FROM a LEFT JOIN b ON a.id = b.id WHERE b.y IS NULL
SELECT * FROM a JOIN b ON a.id = b.id JOIN c ON b.id = c.id
SELECT * FROM (SELECT x FROM t WHERE x > 0) AS sub
SELECT * FROM t WHERE x IN (SELECT y FROM s)
WITH cte AS (SELECT x FROM t) SELECT * FROM cte
SELECT x, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY y ORDER BY z) AS rn FROM t
identity.description: Window function with PARTITION BY and ORDER BY

identity.sql: SELECT 1 UNION ALL SELECT 2
SELECT * FROM t LIMIT 10 OFFSET 5
SELECT CASE WHEN x > 1 THEN 'a' ELSE 'b' END FROM t
SELECT COALESCE(a, b, c) FROM t
SELECT ADD_DAYS(d, 7), NVL(a, b) FROM t
identity.expected: SELECT DATE_ADD('day', 7, d), COALESCE(a, b) FROM t

identity.sql: SELECT SUBSTR(s, 1, 3) FROM t
identity.description: SUBSTR canonicalized to SUBSTRING by parser (Phase 2 transform will restore SUBSTR)

identity.sql: SELECT add_days(d, 7) FROM t
identity.description: Mixed-case function name transformed to DATE_ADD

identity.sql: SELECT * FROM t WHERE x IS NULL
SELECT * FROM t WHERE x IS NOT NULL
SELECT a, b FROM t ORDER BY a, b DESC
SELECT a, b FROM t WHERE x = 1 AND y = 2 OR z = 3
SELECT a, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM t GROUP BY a HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
SELECT * FROM t WHERE x BETWEEN 1 AND 10
SELECT * FROM t WHERE x IN (1, 2, 3)
SELECT * FROM t WHERE x LIKE '%foo%'
SELECT a || b FROM t
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT a) FROM t
SELECT SUM(a), AVG(a), MIN(a), MAX(a) FROM t
WITH cte1 AS (SELECT 1 AS x), cte2 AS (SELECT 2 AS y) SELECT * FROM cte1, cte2
SELECT * FROM t WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM s WHERE s.id = t.id)
identity.expected: SELECT * FROM t WHERE EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM s WHERE s.id = t.id)

identity.sql: SELECT a, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY b ORDER BY c DESC) AS rn FROM t
SELECT a FROM t -- trailing comment
identity.expected: SELECT a FROM t /* trailing comment */
identity.description: Single-line comment preserved as block comment

identity.sql: SELECT a /* inline comment */ FROM t
identity.description: Multi-line comment preserved in identity round-trip

identity.sql: SELECT a FROM t WHERE /* outer /* inner */ x = 1
identity.expected: SELECT a FROM t WHERE x = 1
identity.description: No nested comments: first */ closes the comment

identity.sql: select a, b from t where x > 1
identity.expected: SELECT a, b FROM t WHERE x > 1
